prompt:
Replacing the garage kiosk vendor software before the contract renews. Python 3.12, stdlib only, one JSON file as the db. Non-negotiable module names (the payment terminal imports the first two directly): `cli.py` for the commands, `pricing.py` for fee math (pure functions, zero file IO), `store.py` owning the db format. Add more modules if your design wants them.

Every command is `python3 cli.py --db PATH <command> ...`, each invocation its own process. Times are ALWAYS explicit `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM` arguments, never the wall clock -- disputes get replayed from the gate camera log. Malformed time or size: nonzero exit. Transcript from the whiteboard:

    $ python3 cli.py --db g.json init --compact 40 --standard 60 --oversize 8
    $ python3 cli.py --db g.json enter 7KDJ201 --size compact --time 2026-05-01T08:02
    spot C07
    $ python3 cli.py --db g.json find 7KDJ201
    spot=C07	since=2026-05-01T08:02
    $ python3 cli.py --db g.json exit 7KDJ201 --time 2026-05-01T11:40
    fee $8.00
    $ python3 cli.py --db g.json report
    compact	used=12/40
    standard	used=31/60
    oversize	used=2/8
    revenue=$412.50

Rules:
- `init` creates the spot inventory: ids are class letter + zero-padded two-digit number from 01 (C01..C40, S01.., O01..). Counts below zero, or all three zero: nonzero exit. Running init when the db already exists would orphan live tickets, so refuse (nonzero) and leave the file byte-for-byte alone. Every other command on a missing db: nonzero.
- `enter PLATE --size compact|standard|oversize --time T`: assign the lowest-numbered free spot of the vehicle's own class; if that class is full, upgrade to the next class up (compact -> standard -> oversize, standard -> oversize, oversize has nowhere to go). Nothing free anywhere it fits: nonzero, stderr mentions the size class. A plate that's already inside: nonzero, stderr mentions the plate. Prints exactly `spot ID`.
- `exit PLATE --time T`: prints exactly `fee $X.XX`, frees the spot, adds the fee to lifetime revenue. Unknown/absent plate: nonzero mentioning it. Exit time before entry means a clock fault -- nonzero AND the car stays parked.
- `find PLATE`: exactly `spot=ID	since=ENTRYTIME` (entry time as originally given). Absent plate: nonzero mentioning it.
- `report`: exactly the four lines above -- compact, standard, oversize (occupancy counted by SPOT class, so an upgraded compact shows in the standard row), then `revenue=$X.XX` of everything ever collected.

Pricing -- integer cents only, I've been burned by floats: hourly rate by VEHICLE class (they pay for what they drive, not the spot we gave them): compact $2.00, standard $3.00, oversize $5.00, any fraction of an hour rounds up to a full hour. Daily maximums $12 / $18 / $30. Stays of 15 minutes or less are free, period (grace is on the total stay, not per day). Stays over 24h: each complete 24h block charges the daily max, the remainder is billed hourly and capped at the daily max again. `pricing.fee_cents(entry, exit_, size) -> int` and it must raise ValueError on exit-before-entry or an unknown size. `store.load(path)` / `store.save(path, state)` round-trip the whole db.

Acceptance gate is `python3 test_garage.py` -- it stays exactly as-is.
